models.Training: A structured learning program with slides, a quiz, rules page config, and settings. Displayed in the training list and edited in the builder.
models.Slide: A single content unit within a training. Types: video, text, or question. Ordered sequentially.
models.Question: A quiz question with answer options and a correct answer. Can be a quiz-level question or an inline slide question.
models.Department: An organizational unit used for scoping training assignments.

relationships: Slide may contain one Question (for question-type slides)

content.body:
Welcome to Workplace Safety Training

This training covers the essential safety protocols every team member must know. By the end, you'll understand emergency procedures, equipment handling, and how to report incidents.

Take your time with each section — your safety matters.

content.title: Emergency Evacuation Procedures
content.body: Watch this video to understand our building's evacuation routes, assembly points, and the role of floor wardens.

Know Your Fire Extinguishers

Different fire types require different extinguishers. Using the wrong type can make a fire worse.

  • Class A (Water): Paper, wood, cloth
  • Class B (Foam/CO2): Flammable liquids
  • Class C (CO2/Dry Chemical): Electrical fires
  • Class K (Wet Chemical): Kitchen/cooking fires

When in doubt, evacuate and let the fire department handle it.

content.questionText: Which type of fire extinguisher should you use for an electrical fire?
content.explanation: Electrical fires require CO2 or dry chemical extinguishers. Water-based extinguishers can cause electrocution.

content.body:
How to Report an Incident

All workplace incidents — no matter how minor — must be reported within 24 hours.

Steps:

  1. Ensure the area is safe
  2. Provide first aid if trained to do so
  3. Notify your supervisor immediately
  4. Fill out the Incident Report form in PeopleOS
  5. Cooperate with any follow-up investigation

Remember: reporting isn't about blame — it's about prevention.

content.questionText: Within what timeframe must workplace incidents be reported?
content.explanation: All incidents must be reported within 24 hours, regardless of severity.

quizQuestions.questionText: What is the first thing you should do when you hear the fire alarm?

options.text: Proceed to the nearest emergency exit
Wait for instructions from your manager

quizQuestions.explanation: Always proceed to the nearest emergency exit immediately. Do not stop to collect belongings.

quizQuestions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a responsibility of a floor warden?

options.text: Accounting for personnel at the assembly point

quizQuestions.explanation: Floor wardens direct evacuations and account for personnel. Firefighting is left to trained fire response teams.

quizQuestions.questionText: How often should fire extinguishers be inspected?
quizQuestions.explanation: Fire extinguishers should be visually inspected monthly and professionally serviced annually.

quizQuestions.questionText: When lifting heavy objects, which technique is safest?

options.text: Bend at the waist, keep legs straight
Bend at the knees, keep back straight

quizQuestions.explanation: Always bend at the knees and keep your back straight. Lift with your legs, not your back.

quizQuestions.questionText: What should you include in an incident report?

options.text: Only injuries that required medical attention
The names of witnesses and a detailed description of the event
Only your opinion on who was at fault

quizQuestions.explanation: Incident reports should include who was involved, witnesses, what happened, when, where, and any contributing factors.
